name: VoxClinBench
description: VoxClinBench is a cross-lingual, cross-disease clinical voice biomarker benchmark. v0.4.1 defines 23 subject-disjoint scored evaluation tasks over five existing corpora (Bridge2AI-Voice v3.0, NeuroVoz, SVD, E-DAIC, MODMA) across four languages (English, Spanish, German, Mandarin), plus 1 zero-shot cross-lingual reference row (A24 MODMA Mandarin depression) that is outside the Holm family. The 23 scored tasks comprise 11 within-Bridge2AI rows (A1-A8 physical + A19-A21 psychiatric) and 12 external-cohort rows (A9 NeuroVoz-es Parkinson's; A10-A18 nine SVD-de voice pathologies; A22 E-DAIC-en depression; A23 E-DAIC-en PTSD). B2AI's composite A12 psychiatric_history self-report (v0.3.3) is excluded because its positives are a union of depression / PTSD / ADHD self-reports in the same cohort. A benchmark task specifies (data, labels, metric); training-corpus choice is submission-disclosed metadata, not benchmark-prescribed, so cross-lingual leave-one-dataset-out (LODO) training protocols do not define rows of their own. The benchmark ships subject-ID manifests (or script-based regenerators for license-restricted corpora), an evaluation harness (DeLong AUROC comparison, paired bootstrap, Holm-Bonferroni correction), per-seed prediction archives for reference baselines, a datasheet, and this Croissant metadata. Raw audio is NOT redistributed; users obtain each corpus directly from its upstream provider via the voxbench fetch CLI (credential-gated per upstream DUA). Anonymous submission for NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets Track.

distribution.name: github-repo
distribution.description: Anonymous GitHub mirror of the voxbench evaluation harness and reference baselines.

distribution.name: hf-dataset
distribution.description: Hugging Face Datasets mirror of benchmark manifests, Croissant metadata, datasheet, and reference baseline predictions.

distribution.name: svd-zenodo-archive
distribution.description: Saarbruecken Voice Database (SVD) primary Zenodo archive; ZIPs per German-language pathology cohort. Not redistributed by voxbench; referenced only so submitters can verify the upstream source of the nine SVD pathology rows A10-A18. License CC-BY-4.0 per Zenodo record 16874898.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-vf-paralysis
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort (Saarland recording protocol filename: Rekurrensparese.zip — recurrent laryngeal nerve paresis, the leading etiology of unilateral vocal fold paralysis). Scored row A10. Subject count: n_pos=213, n_neg=300 (shared SVD healthy pool).

distribution.name: svd-pathology-hyperfunctional-dysphonia
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Hyperfunktionelle Dysphonie (hyperfunctional dysphonia). Scored row A11. n_pos=199, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-laryngitis
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Laryngitis. Scored row A12. n_pos=128, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-functional-dysphonia
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Funktionelle Dysphonie (functional dysphonia). Scored row A13. n_pos=108, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-psychogenic-dysphonia
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Psychogene Dysphonie (psychogenic dysphonia). Scored row A14. n_pos=80, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-contact-pachydermia
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Kontaktpachydermie (contact pachydermia). Scored row A15. n_pos=63, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-reinke-edema
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Reinke Ödem (Reinke's edema). Scored row A16. n_pos=54, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-dysodia
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Dysodie (dysodia, singing-voice disorder). Scored row A17. n_pos=54, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: svd-pathology-vf-polyp
distribution.description: SVD German pathology cohort: Stimmlippenpolyp (vocal fold polyp). Scored row A18. n_pos=40, n_neg=300.

distribution.name: tasks-csv
distribution.description: VoxClinBench task registry CSV (23 scored tasks; columns: task_id, corpus, language, family, upstream_license, fetch_command). Generated from voxbench.tasks.list_scorable_tasks().

recordSet.name: tasks
recordSet.description: The 23 scored VoxClinBench tasks (11 within-Bridge2AI A1-A8 + A19-A21 psychiatric, plus 12 external-cohort: A9 NeuroVoz-es PD, A10-A18 nine SVD-de voice pathologies, A22 E-DAIC-en depression, A23 E-DAIC-en PTSD). Subject-disjoint splits across 5 seeds. Excluded from scored registry: A24 modma.depression (zero-shot cross-lingual reference, outside Holm family), former composite A12 b2ai.psychiatric_history (union of A19-A21 positives), former A15 daicwoz.depression (subset of E-DAIC), svd.leukoplakia and svd.presbyphonia (n_pos < 40 scoring threshold).

field.name: task_id
field.description: Task identifier (e.g., b2ai.parkinsons, neurovoz.parkinsons, svd.hyperfunctional_dysphonia, edaic.depression, edaic.ptsd).

field.name: corpus
field.description: Upstream corpus: bridge2ai | neurovoz | svd | edaic | modma.

field.name: upstream_license
field.description: License of the upstream corpus, not of this benchmark repository.

field.name: fetch_command
field.description: CLI command to obtain the upstream audio after credential verification: voxbench fetch <corpus>.

citeAs: Anonymous (2026). VoxClinBench: a cross-lingual, cross-disease clinical voice biomarker benchmark. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets Track (under review).
citation: Anonymous (2026). VoxClinBench: a cross-lingual, cross-disease clinical voice biomarker benchmark. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets Track (under review).
rai:dataCollection: VoxClinBench does not collect new voice data. All five upstream corpora were collected by their respective teams under their own IRB / institutional approvals; see each corpus's canonical citation (documented in the accompanying datasheet).
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking self-supervised speech representations on clinical voice biomarkers.
Auditing cross-lingual generalisation of clinical voice models.
Tracking community progress under a single subject-disjoint protocol.
rai:dataBiases: VoxClinBench mirrors the demographic distribution of its five upstream corpora. English dominates by subject count (~60%); German is strongly represented by the nine SVD pathology rows (A10-A18) which share a single n_neg=300 healthy-subject pool. Sex and age balance vary per disease (e.g., Parkinson's skews older male; several SVD pathologies skew by age). Ethnicity and race are not uniformly reported across upstream corpora and are NOT included in VoxClinBench manifests. Preliminary per-subgroup fairness analyses appear in the companion paper supplementary Section 11.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: This benchmark does NOT redistribute raw audio, demographic metadata, or per-subject diagnostic labels. The subject-ID manifests contain only pseudonymous identifiers issued by each upstream provider; these identifiers alone cannot be used to re-identify individuals without access to the credentialed upstream dataset. NeuroVoz manifests are NOT redistributed as CSV files in v0.4.0 because Zenodo record 10777657 is CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0 and the ND clause forbids redistributing derivatives; the benchmark ships a deterministic splitter script (``voxbench.splits.neurovoz_splitter``) that regenerates the same 80/20 subject split locally from the user's fetched NeuroVoz data.
rai:dataLimitations: Per-task positive subject counts range from 23 to 213, producing wide bootstrap confidence intervals on tasks below n_pos=50 (the T2 diagnostic tier in Table 2 of the companion paper). The nine SVD pathology rows (A10-A18) share a common n_neg=300 healthy-subject pool, which induces correlated baselines across these rows (accounted for via Holm-Bonferroni within Family A). Cross-corpus deltas against out-of-benchmark cohorts (FEMH, DVBIC, ADReSSo) are reported as directional only. The external-cohort rows A9 (NeuroVoz-es), A10-A18 (SVD-de), A22-A23 (E-DAIC-en), and the A24 MODMA-zh cross-lingual validation target exercise cross-population generalisation; training-corpus choice for these rows is submission-disclosed, not benchmark-prescribed. The composite A12 psychiatric_history self-report from Bridge2AI-Voice v3.0 is intentionally excluded from scored tasks because its positive set is the union of depression / PTSD / ADHD / anxiety / bipolar self-reports in the same cohort (A19-A21 in this benchmark) and therefore does not define an independent biomarker task; the label column remains in the upstream tsv and in our training-pipeline auxiliary heads. svd.leukoplakia (n_pos=34) and svd.presbyphonia (n_pos=39) were scoped out in v0.4.0 because they fall below the n_pos >= 40 scoring threshold; svd.presbyphonia additionally carries an age-HC confound (elderly-only cohort) that would produce misleadingly high AUROC.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Three-year maintenance commitment from the first-author institution: quarterly regression tests of voxbench fetch against each upstream provider's credential endpoint, 14-day hot-patch SLA on any API break, semver-tracked task registry with backward-compatible scoring, and signed Zenodo snapshots per release. Pull requests for new baselines and submissions are accepted via the anonymous GitHub mirror.
rai:dataSocialImpact: VoxClinBench is built so that researchers can audit clinical voice biomarker models against subject-disjoint, cross-lingual, multi-disease evaluation. Positive social impact: reduces over-claiming on small in-language cohorts and surfaces the cross-population generalisation gap, both of which currently mask deployment risk in clinical voice AI. Risk to mitigate: per-task AUROC scores can be misread as deployment-ready accuracy. The benchmark therefore ships per-subgroup fairness analyses (Section 11 of the companion paper) and explicit subject-count thresholds (n_pos>=40) to discourage cherry-picking of underpowered tasks.
